title: barbarea vulgaris subsp. vulgaris
intro: Barbarea vulgaris subsp. vulgaris, also known as wild rocket or field penny cress, is a biennial or annual plant belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that grows up to 2 feet tall and has small yellow flowers. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve flatulence, due to its content of glucosinolates, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of consuming wild rocket include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its high content of vitamins A, C, and K, and minerals such as calcium and iron. Wild rocket can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salads, and is also used as a spice to add flavor to various dishes.
